description:
The Office of Congressman Morgan McGarvey is looking for an Intern to serve in our Washington, D.C. office from March 17 through May 16.

The objective of the internship program is for interns to gain as much experience as possible throughout their tenure and feel connected to all staff and constituents they interact with. Interns should leave the program with substantial knowledge of how a congressional office works, why the U.S. House of Representatives is important, and how the federal government functions overall. It is also important for the interns to leave with a staff-approved resume and writing sample.
responsibilities: Leading tours of the Capitol for constituents
Assisting staff with miscellaneous tasks as needed
qualifications: Must be able to commit to at least 20 hours a week for 10 weeks
Must be eligible to be paid by the United States Government
Must meet employment eligibility requirements for the U.S. House of Representatives
Must be able to present proof of valid work authorization in accordance with the Immigration Reform Act of 1986
how_to_apply: To apply for our DC office, send your resume, a cover letter, two references, and a short writing sample (less than 500 words) to Halpin.Burke@mail.house.gov. Please send all application materials as one PDF document with the subject line: 'Last Name, First Name- Spring 2025 Internship'
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer; we do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, military status, gender identity and expression, marital status, parental status, sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

description: Congressman Troy E. Nehls (TX-22) Washington, D.C. office is currently seeking qualified individuals who have a passion for federal policy and America First priorities to serve in his Washington, D.C. office for the Spring of 2025. This internship provides students with a firsthand understanding of our nation's legislative process. Interns will contribute to the daily operations of the office. Duties include greeting visitors, answering phones, leading tours of the Capitol, attending briefings and hearings, performing legislative research, and assisting both legislative and communications staff. Full-time interns will work 9:00 AM to 5:00 PM when out of session, 8:30 AM to 6:00 PM when in session Monday through Friday to meet the full-time rate. Accommodations may be made for students who attend classes during the work week or those with other commitments.
responsibilities: Assist both legislative and communications staff
qualifications: Passion for federal policy and America First priorities
Texas ties preferred but not required
Interest in the legislative process
Ability to work full-time hours for full stipend consideration
how_to_apply: Submit a resume and cover letter to andreas.schnabel@mail.house.gov with 'Spring 2025 Internship' in the subject line. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis with a target start date of February 5th, 2025.
salary_info: Paid internship. Full stipend offered to interns available to work during regular hours of operations. Stipends vary for part-time interns depending on availability/hours per week working. Transportation reimbursement offered for WMATA Metro system commute.

description:
Congresswoman Suzan DelBene, representing the 1st Congressional District of Washington state, seeks interns for her Washington, D.C. and District offices. Interns will work in a fast-paced congressional office, gaining valuable experience and knowledge of the United States Congress.

Intern responsibilities include greeting constituents and handling constituent phone calls, assisting staff with administrative tasks, conducting research, and assisting with constituent correspondence. Kirkland, WA interns will have the opportunity to attend community events and tours with the Congresswoman and staff. Washington, D.C. interns will have the opportunity to lead tours of the U.S. Capitol as well as attend hearings and briefings for the legislative staff.
responsibilities: Greet constituents and handle constituent phone calls
Attend community events and tours with the Congresswoman and staff (Kirkland, WA interns)
Lead tours of the U.S. Capitol and attend hearings and briefings for the legislative staff (Washington, D.C. interns)
qualifications: Possess exceptional written and oral communication skills
Demonstrate a high degree of professionalism
Have a strong interest in public service
Washington state ties are a plus but are not required
how_to_apply: To apply, please submit a cover letter, resume, and short writing sample (1 to 2 pages) through this form. The deadline for Summer 2025 Internship Applications is 11:59 PM on February 24, 2025. Due to a high volume of applications, you will only be contacted if you are selected for an interview. No phone calls or walk ins.
equal_opportunity: This office is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate based on race, religion, disability, age, ethnicity, national origin, sex, gender, LGBTQ identification, family status, or military status. Women, people of color, and other members of traditionally underrepresented communities are strongly encouraged to apply.

description:
The Office of Congressman Brian Mast (R-FL-21) is currently accepting applications for unpaid internships at the Washington, D.C. member office during the spring 2025 term. This internship will run from early February through May.

The ideal candidate will be able to work in a fast-paced environment, have a general understanding of the legislative process, and possess strong written and oral communication skills. Florida ties are not necessary but preferred. The internship schedule is flexible for college students if needed.
responsibilities: Answer and log constituent phone calls
Assist staff with administrative duties and projects
qualifications: Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
General understanding of the legislative process
Strong written and oral communication skills
Florida ties preferred
how_to_apply: Interested applicants should email their resumes and a brief writing sample to joseph.harrington@mail.house.gov. Please use the subject line "LASTNAME - Spring 2025 internship."

office: U.S. House Select Committee on Strategic Competition between the United States and the Chinese Communist Party (Democratic Staff)
description: The Democratic Staff of the U.S. House Select Committee on Strategic Competition between the United States and the Chinese Communist Party seeks qualified undergraduate students and graduates for paid internships beginning as early as January 20th. This position is hybrid (in-office and remote). Interns play a key role in the office and will work closely with Committee counsel and staff. Our ideal candidates are organized, committed, and eager to join a fast-paced environment and build upon their knowledge of how Congress works—in particular, the role that congressional committees play in the federal legislative process. Candidates with diverse backgrounds and experiences are strongly encouraged to apply.
responsibilities: Assisting Committee staff in preparing for congressional hearings
Performing other policy and administrative tasks as necessary
qualifications: Qualified undergraduate students and graduates
Organized, committed, and eager to join a fast-paced environment
Strong organizational, communication, research, and writing skills
Ability to manage multiple tasks and work well under pressure in a team environment
Background and/or interest in subject areas related to the People's Republic of China strongly preferred
how_to_apply: Submit a resume, cover letter, and one writing sample (two pages max.) to SCCDems.resumes@mail.house.gov. Please put "Spring 2025 Intern Application" in the subject line. In your cover letter, please indicate preferred start/end dates for scheduling purposes.
